2605.21777.title: Understanding Perspectives of Patients, Caregivers and Clinicians towards Emerging Collaborative-decision Making Technologies
2605.21777.paper_summary: In pediatrics, patients, caregivers, and clinicians share responsibility for health decisions, but limited collaboration can undermine outcomes. We conducted a qualitative study examining decision-makers perceptions toward collaborative decision-making technologies, including interactive dashboards, VR simulators, and AI voice assistants. Findings reveal differences in user opinions across groups and indicate technology acceptance is linked to users trust of these technologies. Technology developers and researchers need to explore design and implementation strategies that build and facilitate trust or appropriate distrust between users and these novel technologies before these tools can effectively support collaborative decision-making.
2605.21777.paper_summary_zh: 在小兒科中，患者、照護者和臨床醫生共同承擔健康決策的責任，但有限的合作可能會削弱結果。我們進行了一項質性研究，檢視決策者對於協作決策技術的看法，包括互動儀表板、虛擬實境模擬器和人工智慧語音助手。研究結果顯示，各組之間的用戶意見存在差異，並表明技術接受度與用戶對這些技術的信任相關。技術開發者和研究人員需要探索設計和實施策略，以建立和促進用戶與這些新技術之間的信任或適當的不信任，才能使這些工具有效支持協作決策。
2605.21777.author: Ray-Yuan Chung et.al.
2605.21777.authors: Ray-Yuan Chung, Athena Ortega, Zixuan Xu, Daeun Yoo, Jaime Snyder, Wanda Pratt, Aaron Wightman, Ryan Hutson, Cozumel Pruette, Ari Pollack
